## Physical modeling and downscaling framework {background-image="../../shared/assets/images/toulouse-modelers-workshop-2025-wide.jpg" background-size="cover" background-position="center" .section-photo}

<div class="section-title-panel">
<h2>Physical modeling and downscaling framework</h2>
<p>Oceanographic datasets, downscaling approaches, and integration with biological data</p>
</div>

---

## Physical modeling and downscaling framework {background-color="white" .technical-white}

<div class="sb5-header">IKI - Save the Blue 5 (SB5)</div>
<h2 class="slide-title">Physical modeling and downscaling framework</h2>

This section summarizes the ocean products and workflow logic used to connect present-day baselines and future climate conditions to species distribution model predictors

- Contemporary ocean conditions and future climate projections
- Horizontal and vertical products relevant to Blue 5 species
- Delta/change-field downscaling to trusted baseline products
- Curated layers designed for visualization and species distribution model extraction
- Viewer: [Ocean downscaling Shiny app](https://isaakbm-ocean-downscaling-viewer.share.connect.posit.cloud/)

<div class="sb5-footer">isaaksonbm · ibrito@conservation.org</div>

---

## What is a physical climate model? {background-color="white" .technical-white .physical-model-slide}

<div class="sb5-header">IKI - Save the Blue 5 (SB5)</div>
<h2 class="slide-title">What is a physical climate model?</h2>

<div class="two-col-visual">
<div>

Physical models represent the ocean, atmosphere, land, and ice on numerical grids; they simulate energy and mass exchange through time, producing variables that can be summarized for ecological modeling

- horizontal grid
- vertical layers
- physical and biogeochemical processes
- present-day and future time windows

</div>
<div>

![](../../shared/assets/images/climate-model-diagram.png)

</div>
</div>

<div class="sb5-footer">isaaksonbm · ibrito@conservation.org</div>

---

## Downscaling pipeline {background-color="white" .technical-white}

<div class="sb5-header">IKI - Save the Blue 5 (SB5)</div>
<h2 class="slide-title">Downscaling pipeline</h2>

<div class="pipeline">
<div>Trusted baseline ocean products</div>
<div>Model-specific climate change fields</div>
<div>Downscaled future ocean products</div>
<div>Curated layers and exports</div>
<div>Species distribution model predictor extraction</div>
</div>

Current workflow materials are private but documented in the CESM Large Ensemble ocean downscaling repository

<div class="repo-note">Local reference: shared/references/repos/cesmle-ocn-fetch-readme.md</div>

<div class="sb5-footer">isaaksonbm · ibrito@conservation.org</div>

---

## Ocean downscaling viewer {background-color="white" .technical-white .app-demo}

<div class="sb5-header">IKI - Save the Blue 5 (SB5)</div>
<h2 class="slide-title">Ocean downscaling viewer</h2>

<div class="app-grid">
<div>

![](../../shared/assets/screenshots/ocean-downscaling-viewer.png){.screenshot}

</div>
<div>

Use the viewer to inspect:

- variables
- depth layers
- baseline and future products
- spatial patterns
- products feeding the species distribution modeling pipeline

[Open app](https://isaakbm-ocean-downscaling-viewer.share.connect.posit.cloud/)

![](../../shared/assets/qr/ocean-downscaling-viewer-qr.png){.qr}

</div>
</div>

<div class="sb5-footer">isaaksonbm · ibrito@conservation.org</div>

---

## Future work for physical products {background-color="white" .technical-white}

<div class="sb5-header">IKI - Save the Blue 5 (SB5)</div>
<h2 class="slide-title">Future work for physical products</h2>

The current viewer and pipeline provide a transparent foundation; the next step is to expand the ensemble and make uncertainty easier to interpret

- More model families
- More climate scenarios
- More realizations / ensemble members
- More depth-layer and pelagic summaries
- Clearer uncertainty summaries for species distribution model interpretation

<div class="sb5-footer">isaaksonbm · ibrito@conservation.org</div>
